figure.title: Address Caching System
figure.purpose: Illustrates the end-to-end address caching mechanism across Accelerators and Switches, indexed by Destination Accelerator ID (DSTACCID).

technical_summary: Shows a source (SRC) Accelerator communicating with a destination (DST) Accelerator via a Switch.
The SRC Accelerator has a Tx Cache indexed by DSTACCID in its UALink TL.
The Switch has Rx Caches, also indexed by DSTACCID, at its ingress ports.
The DST Accelerator has a Tx Cache (indexed by DSTACCID) for returning responses, and the corresponding Switch port has an Rx Cache.
Green arrows show the flow of cached/compressed address information from the SRC Tx Cache to the Switch Rx Cache, and back from DST Tx to Switch Rx.

components.name: Accelerator Tx Cache
components.description: Cache in the source or destination node used to compress outgoing addresses.

components.name: Switch Rx Cache
components.description: Cache in the switch ingress port to decompress incoming addresses.

critical_facts: Address caching is directional and indexed by the destination Accelerator ID (DSTACCID) to facilitate address compression between adjacent nodes (Accelerator to Switch).
